name: Rao's Famous Lemon Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the broiler for at least 15 minutes before using.
2. Broil the chicken halves, turning once, for about 30 minutes or until the skin is golden-brown and juices run clear when pierced with a fork.
3. Remove the chicken from the broiler and place it on a baking sheet that fits into the broiler. Using a very sharp knife, cut each half into about 6 pieces (leg, thigh, wing, and 3 small breast pieces).
4. Place the chicken on the baking sheet and return to the broiler. Pour the Lemon Sauce over the chicken and toss to coat well. If necessary, divide the sauce in half and do this in two batches.
5. Broil for an additional 3 minutes, turning each piece once more, until lightly browned.
6. Remove from the broiler and portion each chicken onto warm serving plates.
7. Pour the remaining Lemon Sauce into a heavy saucepan over high heat. Stir in parsley and cook for 1 minute. Pour an equal amount of sauce over each chicken and serve with lots of crusty bread to absorb the sauce.
To make Lemon Sauce:
Whisk together juice, oil, vinegar, garlic, oregano, salt, and pepper in a bowl. Cover and refrigerate until ready to use. Whisk or shake vigorously before using.
Serves 6.
